_source: CFA curriculum, Damodaran NYU, CAIA frameworks
_note: Long-tail risk metrics. Most retail investors miss these. Critical for portfolio construction.

interpretation.below_0: Risk-adjusted return negative vs risk-free; reject
interpretation.0_5_to_1: Acceptable; below good hedge fund threshold
interpretation.1_to_2: Good; most well-run equity funds land here
interpretation.above_3: Exceptional; often short timeframe or small strategy

sharpe_ratio.limitation: Assumes normal distribution; understates tail risk in crisis periods

sortino_ratio.difference_from_sharpe: Only penalizes downside volatility (not upside); better for skewed strategies

interpretation.below_1: Too much downside risk relative to return

max_drawdown.interpretation: Max drawdown > 30% strains most portfolios; affects investor behavior (panic selling)
max_drawdown.calmar_ratio: Annual Return / Max Drawdown; > 1.0 is acceptable; > 3.0 is excellent

beta_analysis.alpha: Return above beta-predicted return; Jensen's alpha = R - [Rf + B(Rm-Rf)]
beta_analysis.smart_beta: Factor investing (value, momentum, quality, low-vol) to generate alpha systematically

var_value_at_risk.definition: Maximum expected loss at confidence level over time horizon
var_value_at_risk.example: 1-day 95% VaR of $1M portfolio at 2% = $20,000 max loss 95% of days
var_value_at_risk.cvar_expected_shortfall: Average loss when VaR breach occurs; more conservative; preferred post-2008
limitations: Historical VaR underestimates tail events (black swans)
Correlation breakdown in crisis invalidates diversification assumptions
var_value_at_risk.monte_carlo_approach: Run 10,000+ simulations with fat-tailed distributions (Student-t); more robust

volatility_metrics.implied_volatility: VIX = S&P 500 30-day implied vol; fear gauge

vix_interpretation.below_15: Complacency; corrections often occur in low-vol regimes

volatility_metrics.volatility_clustering: High vol follows high vol (GARCH effect); risk is not constant

portfolio_construction.correlation_matrix_importance: Low/negative correlation between holdings reduces portfolio vol without sacrificing return
portfolio_construction.optimal_number_of_stocks: 20-30 stocks eliminates ~90% of idiosyncratic risk per Markowitz

kelly_criterion.formula: f = (bp - q) / b where b=odds, p=win_prob, q=1-p
kelly_criterion.practitioner_use: Half-Kelly typical; full Kelly requires perfect probability estimates

momentum_factor.note: Highest premium but crashes hard in reversals

factor_investing.quality_factor: Composite of ROE + low accruals + low leverage; most consistent across cycles
factor_investing.low_volatility_anomaly: Low-beta stocks outperform theory (CAPM paradox); attributed to leverage constraints

delta.definition: Rate of change of option price per $1 move in underlying
delta.use_case: Hedge 100 shares: buy 1 put delta=0.50, need 2 contracts to delta-hedge

gamma.definition: Rate of change of delta per $1 move; second derivative
gamma.risk: Long gamma profitable in volatile markets; short gamma (selling options) loses big on moves
gamma.gamma_squeeze: Heavy short option positions force dealers to hedge → amplifies moves (GME, AMC)

theta.definition: Option time decay per day; always negative for option buyer
theta.at_money_behavior: Theta accelerates in final 30 days; steepest last week before expiry
theta.seller_advantage: Selling options = positive theta; collecting time premium systematically

vega.definition: Sensitivity to 1% change in implied volatility
vega.long_vega: Benefits from vol expansion (buying calls/puts before earnings)
vega.short_vega: Benefits from vol compression (selling after earnings; IV crush typical)

options_greeks.implied_vol_skew: Puts trade richer than calls (downside protection premium); 25-delta put IV typically 3-8 vol points above calls
